2.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
2.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

9.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
9.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

14.gl: glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

18.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
18.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
18.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);
